tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From crash to century: Tazmin Brits inspires a nation
stories.summary: Tazmin Brits, told she might never walk again after a car crash 14 years ago, scored 114 runs for South Africa at the Women's T20 World Cup in Bristol, turning heartbreak into triumph.

stories.headline: Neighbors rally to buy beloved delivery driver a new car
stories.summary: Eighty-five neighbors rallied to raise £3,620 for Ann West, a 59-year-old delivery driver of 20 years who couldn't afford to repair her broken car, restoring her joy and livelihood.

stories.headline: Wireless implant gives woman with ALS her voice back
stories.summary: A woman with motor neuron disease regained her voice through a groundbreaking wireless brain implant at University of Michigan Health, offering hope to countless others who've lost speech.

stories.headline: Twelve sea turtles swim home after six months of care
stories.summary: Caregiver Kristen Luise released Kermit, a loggerhead sea turtle she nursed through pneumonia and severe frostbite, among twelve rescued turtles returning home to the ocean.

stories.headline: New cafe opens with 5,000 free cups of joy for neighbors
stories.summary: Josh and Lynn Marshall's new Monarch Community Cafe in Rogers Park gave away 5,000 free cups of coffee on opening week, creating the neighborhood gathering space their community desperately needed.

stories.headline: Neymar returns to the pitch, tears and triumph
stories.summary: Neymar, returning after eight weeks with a calf injury, came off the bench in Brazil's 3-0 World Cup victory and wept looking at his family, knowing they'd fought the journey together.

stories.headline: Business replaces struggling family's window with warmth and grace
stories.summary: KT Fairglass replaced a struggling family's $3,000 window at no cost through their Community Give Back program, bringing warmth to their neurodivergent children's home.

stories.headline: Captain America is back: Pulisic leads Team USA forward
stories.summary: The stadium erupted as Christian Pulisic returned from injury, his first touches proving he's ready to captain Team USA into the future.

stories.headline: Volunteers pack 1,000 care kits to cool hearts in Phoenix heat
stories.summary: Young volunteers and families assembled 1,000 care kits with cooling towels and essentials for homeless neighbors facing Phoenix's scorching summer heat.

stories.headline: Rarest deer in the world bounces back: tamaraw population grows 12%
stories.summary: The critically endangered Philippine tamaraw population rose to 425—a 12% gain through coordinated conservation—bringing hope to one of Earth's most precious wild mammals.

stories.headline: AI discovers new cancer fighter in weeks, not years
stories.summary: University of Pennsylvania researchers used AI to identify GPNMB as a cancer-fighting target in weeks—not years—accelerating hope for patients with multiple cancer types.

stories.headline: Seven rescued primates return home after escaping traffickers
stories.summary: Seven endangered Golden Langurs rescued from wildlife traffickers were released back into Sikhna Jwhwlao National Park, a triumph of rehabilitation and zero-tolerance enforcement.

stories.headline: Paint your walls any color—and cool your home without power
stories.summary: Scientists created a vibrant, plant-based cooling paint that keeps buildings 9°C cooler while reflecting heat, proving sustainable beauty and efficiency can coexist.

stories.headline: Scientists unlock nature's antibiotic megacluster to beat superbugs
stories.summary: McMaster researchers discovered a "megacluster" of four natural antibiotics working together to starve drug-resistant bacteria by targeting biotin, offering a promising new weapon against superbugs.
